title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution on Abolishing Abortion!
author: James Silberman
orientation: On November 16, 2021, Oklahoma Baptists became the fourth Southern Baptist state convention that year to adopt a resolution calling for the immediate abolition of abortion, after messengers approved key amendments clarifying that the call was for abolition without exception or compromise. The article details the floor debate over Blake Gideon's clarifying amendment and Dusty Deevers' speech, and the implications for Oklahoma Baptists' new leadership.
audiences: pro-life Christians weighing incrementalism vs. abolition

questions.question: Which state Baptist conventions had passed resolutions calling for the immediate abolition of abortion before Oklahoma Baptists did so in November 2021?
questions.answer: Oklahoma Baptists were the fourth Southern Baptist state convention in 2021 to pass such a resolution, following Arkansas, South Carolina, and Utah-Idaho, all of which built on the Southern Baptist Convention's June 2021 Resolution on Abolishing Abortion.
questions.quote: On Tuesday, Oklahoma Baptists became the fourth Southern Baptists state convention this year to pass a resolution calling for the immediate abolition of abortion following [Arkansas](https://freethestates.org/2021/10/arkansas-baptists-pass-resolution-on-abolishing-abortion-at-state-convention/index.html), [South Carolina](https://freethestates.org/2021/11/south-carolina-baptist-convention-passes-resolution-abolishing-abortion/index.html), [Utah-Idaho](https://freethestates.org/2021/10/utah-idaho-baptist-convention-calls-ut-id-governors-legislature-to-abolish-abortion/index.html).

questions.question: What was the most consequential amendment added to the Oklahoma Baptists' 2021 resolution, and who introduced it?
questions.answer: Immediate past convention president Blake Gideon offered an amendment making explicit that what was being called for was the immediate end and abolition of abortion, without exception or compromise; he argued the language was needed to reflect the intent of the 2019 Oklahoma and 2021 SBC resolutions.
questions.quote: The most important amendment is the final one — making clear that what is being called for is the immediate abolition of abortion. Blake Gideon, the convention's immediate past president, offered the amendment

questions.question: Why did Blake Gideon argue that scriptural clarity required strengthening the resolution's language?
questions.answer: Gideon told the convention that the Bible models clarity and strength, and that resolutions should reflect the tone of scripture; he said the cultural moment did not allow tiptoeing around abortion, citing that 20 children would be murdered in Oklahoma that day.
questions.quote: We live in a day where there's a culture of death all around us, and we can't tiptoe around issues. We must be clear and we must be strong. 20 children today will be murdered in the state of Oklahoma.

questions.question: What two arguments did the Resolutions Committee, through Troy Taylor, give against Gideon's clarifying amendment?
questions.answer: Speaking for the committee, Comanche Cotton Baptist Association Missions Director Troy Taylor argued the added language was redundant and unnecessary, and that it did not reflect the spectrum of views on abortion within the convention.
questions.quote:
Speaking for the Resolutions Committee against Gideon's amendment, Comanche Cotton Baptist Association Missions Director Troy Taylor argued that:

1. the added language was redundant and unnecessary, and
2. the added language does not reflect the spectrum of views on abortion within the convention.

questions.question: How did James Silberman rebut the Resolutions Committee's claim that Gideon's amendment was redundant?
questions.audience: pro-life Christians weighing incrementalism vs. abolition
questions.answer: Silberman argued that the existence of non-abolitionists within Oklahoma Baptists is precisely why clearer language was needed, and that the committee's own opposition proved the amendment's necessity, since incrementalism is what keeps abortion legal in Oklahoma.
questions.quote: The fact that there are non-abolitionists within Oklahoma Baptists is precisely why the clearer language is necessary. The amendment would only be redundant and unnecessary if there was already uniform agreement on the subject. Beyond that, the Committee's own opposition to the amendment is further proof of the resolution's necessity. Incrementalism and incrementalist politicians are the only things keeping abortion legal in Oklahoma. That ideology is what needs to be specified and rooted out.

questions.question: How did Dusty Deevers describe the unifying line of the 2019 Oklahoma and 2021 SBC resolutions?
questions.answer: As one of the primary authors of both resolutions, Deevers told messengers that the unifying, prophetic, and biblical call of both was the urgent line that called for the abolition of abortion immediately without exception or compromise.
questions.quote: As one of the primary authors of the 2019 amendment that was presented to Oklahoma Baptists, and then the 2021 SBC Resolution on Abolishing Abortion that was passed this summer, I can tell you that the unifying, prophetic, and Biblical call that united both the 2019 Oklahoma Baptists and the 2021 SBC was that urgent line that called for the abolition of abortion immediately without exception or compromise…

questions.question: How did Dusty Deevers frame the biblical command against murder in relation to abortion laws and court rulings?
questions.audience: pro-life Christians weighing incrementalism vs. abolition
questions.answer: Deevers preached that God's word commands 'you shall not murder' rather than 'you shall regulate murder,' and that any court opinion or law permitting the killing of preborn children defies God and must be abolished.
questions.quote: God's Word commands 'you shall not murder.' If a court writes opinions or decrees, as those in Isaiah 10:1-2, that says women have the right to murder their children, that court is defying God and has blood on their hands. If any law is passed in any state saying when, where and how you can murder a baby, those laws must be abolished as God commands. God's Word does not say 'you shall regulate murder.' It says 'you shall not murder.'

questions.question: What historical comparison did Deevers draw to convey the scale of abortion since Roe v. Wade?
questions.answer: Deevers told messengers that abortion in the United States over the past 50 years was ten times greater than Hitler's final solution against the Jews, and that Oklahomans send over 5,000 preborn citizens to slaughter every year.
questions.quote: Brothers and sisters, the vicious holocaust against our preborn neighbors in the United States over the past 50 years since Roe v. Wade is ten times greater than Hitler's final solution against the Jews. Oklahomans lead over 5,000 preborn citizens to slaughter every year.

questions.question: How wide was the margin by which Gideon's clarifying amendment passed?
questions.answer: After Pastor Brandon Allen of Ekklesia Muskogee called the convention to a vote, the overwhelming majority supported Gideon's amendment, with Deevers reporting that roughly three-fourths of the messengers voted in favor.
questions.quote: Pastor Brandon Allen of Ekklesia Muskogee then called the convention to a vote, and the overwhelming majority supported Gideon's amendment. According to Deevers, roughly three-fourths of the messengers voted in favor.

questions.question: What additional amendment did David Dunsworth offer, and how did it fare?
questions.answer: David Dunsworth of Northside Baptist Church motioned to amend the resolution by adding language affirming that life begins at conception and inserting the word 'preborn,' and the messengers affirmed his motion.
questions.quote: David Dunsworth of Northside Baptist Church then motioned to amend the resolution by adding language about life beginning at conception, and adding the word "preborn;" a motion which was affirmed by the messengers.

questions.question: What procedural attempt did Mike Keahbone make against the amendments, and what was the ruling?
questions.answer: Lawton First Baptist Church Pastor Mike Keahbone moved to cancel all of the amendments, calling the debate over semantics and redundancy ridiculous; his motion was ruled out of order because amendments already adopted cannot be canceled.
questions.quote: Lawton First Baptist Church Pastor and former Oklahoma Baptists First Vice President Mike Keahbone attempted a motion to undo all of the amendments, saying, "To sit here and argue over semantics and redundancy is ridiculous. I motion that we cancel all the amendments." His motion was ruled out of order, as amendments already adopted cannot be canceled.

questions.question: How did Pastor Tom Fortner respond to the charge that the convention was arguing over mere semantics?
questions.answer: Mill Creek First Baptist Church Pastor Tom Fortner argued that the work was not semantic but a matter of clarifying the resolution for a lost and dying world that habitually plays word games and distorts reports of Baptist life.
questions.quote: We are not arguing over semantics. We are looking to clarify a statement for a lost and dying world. The world around us plays word games with what we say. If you look at how they report events that occur in Baptist life, you will see it distorted beyond recognition. We need to be absolutely clear in how we say what we say.

questions.question: What earlier real-world example did the article cite to demonstrate that abolitionist resolutions get distorted without clear language?
questions.answer: After the 2019 Oklahoma Baptists resolution passed, Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat — described as the primary opponent of abolishing abortion in Oklahoma — told the Muskogee Politico that the abolitionist resolution was no big deal because the convention was simply standing for life as it always had.
questions.quote: Yet, Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat — a pro-life professing Christian, and the primary opponent of abolishing abortion in Oklahoma — [commented to the *Muskogee Politico*](https://www.muskogeepolitico.com/2019/11/senate-president-treat-no-change-in.html) that the new abolitionist resolution was no big deal because the convention was simply standing for life as it always had.

questions.question: What concrete obligations does the article say new Oklahoma Baptists President Chris Wall and Executive Director Todd Fisher now have?
questions.answer: The article argues Wall and Fisher must loudly speak on behalf of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, stop championing incremental bills and incrementalist politicians at Rose Day, and partner with abolitionist organizations rather than incrementalist Roman Catholic figures.
questions.quote: This means loudly speaking on behalf of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act. It means no more championing incremental bills and incrementalist politicians at Rose Day. It means partnering with Oklahoma-based abolitionist organizations like Rescue Those, Liberty Rising, Southern Baptists for Abolishing Abortion, and Free the States instead of incrementalist Roman Catholics like Abby Johnson, Tony Lauinger, and Brett Farley.

questions.question: What sermon and scripture did Deevers invoke to call messengers to fearless unity?
questions.answer: Deevers referenced Pastor Michael Butler's sermon on Philippians 1:27-30, urging messengers to walk in a manner worthy of the gospel and stand firm with fearless unity, and quoted the 2021 SBC Resolution's call for pastors and entity leaders to use their gifts to abolish abortion immediately without exception or compromise.
questions.quote: As we were exhorted from Pastor Michael Butler's sermon on Philippians 1:27-30, because we understand the urgency, we must unite to walk in a manner worthy of the gospel; standing firm with fearless unity. I echo and quote a line from the 2021 Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion: 'We call upon pastors and entity leaders to use their God-given gifts of preaching, teaching, and leading with one unified, principled, prophetic voice to abolish abortion immediately without exception or compromise.'

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: The 2019 Oklahoma Baptists resolution this 2021 resolution explicitly reaffirms and clarifies.

related.title: Abolitionists Pass Resolution, Make Huge Strides at SBC Convention
related.reason: The June 2021 SBC Resolution on Abolishing Abortion is the parent national resolution this Oklahoma vote echoes.

related.reason: One of the three sibling state conventions that passed an abolition resolution in 2021 before Oklahoma.

related.reason: Another 2021 sibling state-convention resolution cited as part of the same wave.

related.reason: The third 2021 sibling state-convention resolution that preceded Oklahoma's vote.

related.reason: Background on the SBC resolution co-authored by Dusty Deevers, who spoke at the Oklahoma convention.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Provides context on the incrementalist Oklahoma figures (including Greg Treat) the article critiques.
